channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: OITS 2026 Policy Fellowship Applications Open: A Unique Opportunity for UK Civil Servants to Collaborate with Leading Academics and Shape Evidence-Based Policy
item.dc:creator: Hope N. Magwira
item.description:
The Office for Institutional and Thematic Studies (OITS), through its Policy Insights Unit (PIU), has officially opened applications for the highly anticipated 2026 Policy Fellowship Programme. This prestigious fellowship offers UK-based civil servants and public officials an opportunity to deepen their policy expertise, work closely with leading academics, and develop research that can contribute to […]

The post OITS 2026 Policy Fellowship Applications Open: A Unique Opportunity for UK Civil Servants to Collaborate with Leading Academics and Shape Evidence-Based Policy appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Office for Institutional and Thematic Studies (OITS), through its Policy Insights Unit (PIU), has officially opened applications for the highly anticipated 2026 Policy Fellowship Programme. This prestigious fellowship offers UK-based civil servants and public officials an opportunity to deepen their policy expertise, work closely with leading academics, and develop research that can contribute to better policymaking across government.

The fellowship is designed to strengthen the relationship between academia and government by enabling policymakers to engage directly with cutting-edge research while developing practical solutions to real-world policy challenges.

Application Deadline: 15 July 2026

What is the OITS 2026 Policy Fellowship?

The OITS 2026 Policy Fellowship is a six-month programme that brings together civil servants and academic experts from leading UK universities. During the fellowship period, participants will undertake policy-focused research under the guidance of an academic mentor and produce a research output relevant to their area of policy responsibility.

The programme aims to bridge the gap between academic knowledge and public policy implementation, ensuring that government decisions are informed by high-quality evidence and innovative thinking.

This initiative represents the fourth edition of the fellowship programme and builds upon the success of previous cohorts. In 2025, twenty civil servants from twelve government departments participated in the programme, collaborating with academics to produce impactful policy research and recommendations.

Fellowship Partner Universities

Successful fellows will receive mentorship from academics affiliated with some of the United Kingdom’s respected higher education institutions, including:

  • Brunel University London
  • Lancaster University
  • University of Surrey
  • University of York

These institutions provide expertise across a broad range of disciplines and policy areas, ensuring fellows receive relevant guidance tailored to their research interests.

Research Areas Available

The 2026 fellowship offers participants the opportunity to explore an expanded range of policy themes and research areas.

Areas of interest include, but are not limited to:

1. Health and Wellbeing

Participants may investigate public health challenges, healthcare delivery systems, mental health policies, community wellbeing initiatives, and strategies for improving health outcomes.

2. Sustainability and Net Zero

Research may focus on climate policy, environmental sustainability, green transitions, renewable energy, and pathways toward achieving net-zero targets.

3. Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technologies

Fellows can explore how technological advancements are shaping society, governance, regulation, innovation, and economic development.

4. Governance and Public Administration

Research opportunities exist around public sector reform, institutional effectiveness, policymaking processes, accountability, and public service delivery.

5. Global Systems and International Relationships

Participants may investigate international cooperation, geopolitical developments, trade relationships, and global governance challenges.

6. Education and Social Justice

Potential topics include educational equity, social mobility, inclusion, workforce development, and policies aimed at reducing inequalities.

7. Creative and Cultural Futures

Research may examine the role of arts, culture, creative industries, and cultural policy in economic and social development.

8. Economic Development

Participants can focus on economic growth, productivity, innovation ecosystems, labour markets, entrepreneurship, and regional development.

Benefits of the OITS 2026 Policy Fellowship

The fellowship provides numerous professional and personal development opportunities for participants.

Academic Mentorship

Fellows will receive direct support from leading academics with specialist expertise relevant to their policy area.

Enhanced Research Skills

Participants will strengthen their abilities in:

  • Data analysis
  • Evidence interpretation
  • Policy evaluation
  • Research design
  • Knowledge synthesis

Expanded Professional Networks

The programme creates valuable connections between:

  • Civil servants
  • Government departments
  • Academic institutions
  • Policy researchers
  • Subject matter experts

Increased Subject Expertise

Participants gain deeper knowledge of their policy area and access to the latest academic research and evidence.

Improved Policy Impact

By integrating academic insight into policymaking, fellows are better positioned to develop innovative and evidence-based solutions to public challenges.

Who Should Apply?

The programme is seeking applications from UK-based civil servants and public officials who wish to strengthen their policy knowledge and engage more deeply with research.

Applicants should have a strong interest in evidence-informed policymaking and be prepared to dedicate time to conducting policy-related research during the fellowship period.

The fellowship is particularly suitable for individuals looking to:

  • Improve policy development skills
  • Expand professional networks
  • Strengthen analytical capabilities
  • Collaborate with academic experts
  • Produce impactful policy research

Application Process

Applications for the OITS 2026 Policy Fellowship are now open, and the application form is available online.

Interested candidates should:

  1. Review the official Fellowship Q&A document.
  2. Carefully examine eligibility requirements.
  3. Complete the official application form.
  4. Submit all required materials before the deadline.

Applicants with questions regarding the fellowship can contact the programme team through the official fellowship communication channels provided by the Policy Insights Unit.

Important Deadline

Application Deadline: Wednesday, 15 July 2026

Prospective applicants are encouraged to begin preparing their applications early to ensure sufficient time for reviewing eligibility criteria, developing research ideas, and completing all application requirements.

Why This Fellowship Matters

Modern governments face increasingly complex challenges that require evidence-based and interdisciplinary solutions. The OITS 2026 Policy Fellowship provides a valuable platform for policymakers to engage directly with academic expertise and generate research-driven insights that can improve public policy outcomes.

By fostering collaboration between government and academia, the fellowship contributes to more informed decision-making, stronger policy development, and better public services. For civil servants seeking to expand their expertise and make a meaningful impact within government, this programme represents an exceptional professional development opportunity.

Discover more global fellowship opportunities on OFY here

The post OITS 2026 Policy Fellowship Applications Open: A Unique Opportunity for UK Civil Servants to Collaborate with Leading Academics and Shape Evidence-Based Policy appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Youth Advisory Squad (YAS) Leadership Accelerator Fellowship 2026–2027 Applications Open: Earn a $500 Scholarship, Develop Leadership Skills, and Become a Youth Advocate Against Child Sexual Exploitation
item.dc:creator: Hope N. Magwira
item.description:
Applications are now open for the Youth Advisory Squad (YAS) Leadership Accelerator Fellowship 2026–2027, a transformative virtual youth leadership program designed to empower young people with the knowledge, skills, and opportunities needed to become effective advocates, advisors, and changemakers within their communities. Organized by OneChild, the Youth Advisory Squad (YAS) is a free virtual scholarship […]

The post Youth Advisory Squad (YAS) Leadership Accelerator Fellowship 2026–2027 Applications Open: Earn a $500 Scholarship, Develop Leadership Skills, and Become a Youth Advocate Against Child Sexual Exploitation appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the Youth Advisory Squad (YAS) Leadership Accelerator Fellowship 2026–2027, a transformative virtual youth leadership program designed to empower young people with the knowledge, skills, and opportunities needed to become effective advocates, advisors, and changemakers within their communities.

Organized by OneChild, the Youth Advisory Squad (YAS) is a free virtual scholarship and empowerment program that equips young people with leadership skills while engaging them in the global effort to end the sexual exploitation of children. The fellowship provides a unique opportunity for youth to gain practical advocacy experience, receive professional mentorship, and contribute to meaningful social change.

Young people who are passionate about community development, child protection, leadership, and advocacy are encouraged to apply before the application deadline.

Application Deadline: August 15, 2026

What is the Youth Advisory Squad (YAS) Leadership Accelerator Fellowship?

The Youth Advisory Squad (YAS) is a youth-centered leadership development initiative that seeks to engage and empower young leaders to become ambassadors, advisors, and advocates within their communities and beyond.

The fellowship aims to provide participants with the tools and knowledge necessary to understand and address issues related to the sexual exploitation of children while developing leadership and professional skills that can support their future educational and career goals.

Through a structured virtual learning experience, fellows actively participate in discussions, training sessions, advocacy activities, and community projects designed to create real-world impact.

Program Duration and Structure

The YAS Leadership Accelerator Fellowship runs from September 2026 to June 2027.

The program is entirely virtual, making it accessible to participants regardless of their location.

Key program features include:

  • Virtual participation from home
  • Biweekly meetings held every other Saturday
  • Interactive leadership development sessions
  • Expert-led training workshops
  • Community engagement opportunities
  • Collaborative learning with fellow youth leaders

This flexible online format allows participants to balance their educational commitments while actively engaging in the fellowship experience.

What Participants Will Learn

The fellowship provides extensive training in leadership and advocacy-related areas that are essential for young changemakers.

Participants will receive training in:

1. Advocacy Skills

Fellows will learn how to advocate effectively for issues affecting children and youth while influencing positive change within their communities.

2. Public Speaking

Participants will develop confidence and communication skills necessary for speaking in public settings, presentations, meetings, and advocacy campaigns.

3. Facilitation Skills

The program teaches youth how to lead discussions, coordinate group activities, and facilitate meaningful conversations within community and organizational settings.

4. Action Planning

Fellows will learn how to design and implement practical projects that address social issues and create measurable impact.

5. Child Protection Awareness

Participants will gain a deeper understanding of:

  • Sexual exploitation of children
  • Child protection challenges
  • Prevention strategies
  • Community awareness approaches
  • Youth participation in safeguarding efforts

Capstone Community Project

One of the most important components of the fellowship is the mandatory capstone project.

Each participant will be required to design and implement a project within their school or community that addresses issues connected to child protection, youth advocacy, awareness creation, or social impact.

The capstone project allows fellows to apply the knowledge and leadership skills gained throughout the fellowship while making a direct contribution to their communities.

Learn from Industry Experts and Guest Speakers

Throughout the fellowship, participants will engage with guest speakers and professionals from various sectors.

These experts provide valuable insights into:

  • Leadership development
  • Youth engagement
  • Advocacy campaigns
  • Social impact initiatives
  • Community organizing
  • Career development pathways

Exposure to experienced professionals helps participants expand their networks and gain practical knowledge that can support their future aspirations.

Opportunity to Shape Youth Engagement Strategies

An important aspect of the fellowship is the opportunity for participants to contribute directly to OneChild’s youth engagement initiatives.

Fellows will:

  • Share perspectives and experiences
  • Provide feedback on youth programs
  • Offer recommendations for future initiatives
  • Help strengthen youth-focused strategies

This advisory role ensures that young voices remain central to decision-making processes affecting youth programs.

Benefits of the YAS Leadership Accelerator Fellowship

Successful completion of the fellowship comes with several valuable benefits.

Financial Support

Each fellow who successfully completes the program receives:

A $500 Scholarship

This scholarship serves as recognition of the fellow’s commitment, participation, and contribution throughout the program.

Professional Recognition

Graduates receive:

  • A personalized letter of reference
  • A certificate of completion

These credentials can strengthen future scholarship, internship, educational, and employment applications.

Internship Opportunities

Top-performing fellows and alumni gain access to:

Competitive and limited paid Summer Internship opportunities with OneChild.

These internships provide additional professional experience and career development opportunities.

Leadership and Career Development

Participants also benefit from:

  • Enhanced leadership skills
  • Increased confidence
  • Expanded professional networks
  • Community engagement experience
  • Career readiness development
  • Real-world advocacy experience

Why You Should Apply

The Youth Advisory Squad Leadership Accelerator Fellowship is more than a leadership program. It is an opportunity for young people to become active participants in addressing important social challenges while developing skills that will benefit them throughout their academic and professional journeys.

By joining the fellowship, participants gain valuable leadership experience, build meaningful connections, contribute to child protection efforts, and position themselves for future educational and career opportunities.

Application Deadline

Applications are currently open and will close on:

August 15, 2026

Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications as early as possible because interviews will be scheduled immediately after applications are received.

How to Apply

Interested applicants can submit their applications online through the official application portal.

Prospective fellows should carefully complete their applications and prepare for the interview process following submission.

Discover more global accelerator programs on OFY here

The post Youth Advisory Squad (YAS) Leadership Accelerator Fellowship 2026–2027 Applications Open: Earn a $500 Scholarship, Develop Leadership Skills, and Become a Youth Advocate Against Child Sexual Exploitation appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: CDEMA Young Professionals Programme (YPP) 2026: Fully Supported Caribbean Disaster Management Internship for Young Graduates Now Open
item.dc:creator: Hope N. Magwira
item.description:
The Caribbean Disaster Emergency Management Agency (CDEMA) has officially announced applications for its highly anticipated Young Professionals Programme (YPP) 2026, offering young graduates across participating Caribbean states a unique opportunity to gain practical experience, build professional skills, and contribute to disaster risk management and resilience-building efforts throughout the region. The internship programme is designed to […]

The post CDEMA Young Professionals Programme (YPP) 2026: Fully Supported Caribbean Disaster Management Internship for Young Graduates Now Open appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Caribbean Disaster Emergency Management Agency (CDEMA) has officially announced applications for its highly anticipated Young Professionals Programme (YPP) 2026, offering young graduates across participating Caribbean states a unique opportunity to gain practical experience, build professional skills, and contribute to disaster risk management and resilience-building efforts throughout the region.

The internship programme is designed to empower the next generation of disaster management professionals by providing hands-on learning experiences, mentorship opportunities, and exposure to regional disaster management initiatives. Through this programme, CDEMA aims to strengthen youth participation and leadership in Comprehensive Disaster Management (CDM) while creating pathways for future employment and professional growth.

Successful candidates will be based at the CDEMA Headquarters in Bridgetown, Barbados, for a period of 12 to 18 months and will receive a monthly stipend along with relocation support for international participants.

Application Deadline: July 3, 2026

About the Caribbean Disaster Emergency Management Agency (CDEMA)

The Caribbean Disaster Emergency Management Agency (CDEMA) is the regional inter-governmental agency responsible for coordinating disaster management initiatives among participating Caribbean states.

CDEMA works to enhance the capacity of Caribbean countries to effectively manage hazards, reduce disaster risks, and build resilient communities. Through its Comprehensive Disaster Management strategy, the organization promotes sustainable development, preparedness, response, recovery, and resilience-building across the region.

Recognizing the critical role of young people in shaping resilient societies, CDEMA has developed the Young Professionals Programme to cultivate future leaders within the disaster risk management sector.

What is the Young Professionals Programme (YPP)?

The Young Professionals Programme (YPP) is an internship initiative established to provide opportunities for recent graduates from post-secondary institutions while building the skills and competencies necessary for careers in disaster management and resilience planning.

The programme seeks to:

  • Develop future disaster management professionals
  • Promote youth leadership in resilience-building efforts
  • Encourage innovation in disaster risk management
  • Support entrepreneurship within the disaster management sector
  • Strengthen regional disaster preparedness and response capacity
  • Build a skilled workforce capable of supporting Caribbean states during emergencies and recovery efforts

The programme follows a practical learning-by-doing approach that enables participants to gain real-world experience while contributing to meaningful projects and initiatives.

Internship Location and Duration

Selected interns will be based at the CDEMA Headquarters located in Bridgetown, Barbados.

Key programme details include:

  • Location: Bridgetown, Barbados
  • Internship Duration: 12 to 18 months
  • Organization: Caribbean Disaster Emergency Management Agency (CDEMA)
  • Competition Reference Number: CDEMA-CU/YPP/03
  • Application Deadline: July 3, 2026

Applicants should be available for the entire internship period.

Areas of Professional Development

Throughout the programme, interns will gain valuable experience in several critical areas of disaster management and resilience planning.

Participants will have opportunities to work on:

1. Applied Disaster Risk Knowledge

Interns will strengthen their understanding of disaster risk reduction strategies and hazard management approaches.

2. Preparedness and Response Planning

Participants will gain practical experience supporting emergency preparedness and disaster response initiatives.

3. Community Resilience Planning

The programme exposes interns to strategies that strengthen community resilience and disaster preparedness at the local level.

4. Recovery Planning and Implementation

Interns will learn how governments and organizations coordinate recovery efforts following disasters.

5. Climate Change and Development Integration

Participants will gain insights into integrating climate resilience and disaster risk reduction into national and sector planning processes.

6. Project Administration

Interns will develop project management and administrative skills essential for careers in disaster management and development sectors.

Financial Benefits and Support

One of the most attractive aspects of the Young Professionals Programme is the financial and logistical support provided to successful candidates.

Benefits include:

Monthly Stipend

Successful interns will receive a monthly stipend during their placement.

Relocation Assistance

Candidates selected from outside Barbados will receive:

  • A return airfare ticket
  • A settlement grant to support relocation expenses

This support enables participants from across the Caribbean region to take advantage of the opportunity regardless of their location.

Eligibility Requirements

To be considered for the programme, applicants must meet specific eligibility criteria.

Age Requirement

Applicants must be:

  • Between 21 and 30 years old

Nationality Requirement

Applicants must be nationals or citizens of CDEMA Participating States.

Academic Qualifications

Applicants should possess:

  • A first degree in Earth Science, Social Science, Disaster Management, or a related field

Preferred Experience

The following will be considered an advantage:

  • Two years of subject-matter experience
  • Experience within disaster management organizations
  • Experience within civil defense agencies
  • Experience with affiliated organizations working in resilience, emergency response, or disaster risk reduction

Required Skills and Competencies

CDEMA is seeking highly motivated candidates who demonstrate strong technical and interpersonal abilities.

Desired skills include:

  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite
  • Knowledge of project management principles
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills in English
  • Research and analytical skills
  • Ability to understand technical documentation
  • Strong stakeholder engagement abilities
  • Flexibility and adaptability
  • Problem-solving capabilities
  • Data acquisition and analysis skills
  • Attention to detail
  • Critical thinking abilities
  • Teamwork and collaboration skills
  • Initiative and willingness to learn
  • Appreciation for workplace diversity
  • Strong interpersonal skills

These competencies will help participants succeed in a dynamic regional working environment.

How to Apply

Interested applicants should prepare and submit the following documents:

  1. A Statement of Interest (maximum two pages)
  2. Two reference letters
  3. Copies of certificates and academic transcripts

The Statement of Interest should clearly outline:

  • Academic qualifications
  • Relevant subject knowledge
  • Professional experience
  • Career aspirations
  • Interest in disaster management and resilience-building

Applications should be emailed to:

hr@cdema.org

Applicants must quote the competition reference number:

CDEMA-CU/YPP/03

in the subject line of the email.

Why You Should Apply

The CDEMA Young Professionals Programme is an exceptional opportunity for young graduates interested in disaster management, climate resilience, emergency response, community development, and sustainable development.

Participants will gain valuable regional experience, expand their professional networks, develop highly sought-after technical skills, and contribute to building safer and more resilient Caribbean communities. For aspiring professionals seeking a meaningful and impactful career path, the YPP offers an outstanding platform to launch a successful future in disaster risk management and resilience leadership.

Discover more global internship programs on OFY here

The post CDEMA Young Professionals Programme (YPP) 2026: Fully Supported Caribbean Disaster Management Internship for Young Graduates Now Open appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: PBI-USA Solidarity Fellowship 2026: A Unique Human Rights Fellowship for Emerging Leaders Committed to Global Justice
item.dc:creator: Hope N. Magwira
item.description:
Are you passionate about human rights, social justice, advocacy, grassroots activism, and international solidarity? The PBI-USA Solidarity Fellowship offers an exceptional opportunity for emerging leaders to deepen their understanding of human rights protection while supporting the work of frontline human rights defenders around the world. The Solidarity Fellowship is a leadership and advocacy initiative organized […]

The post PBI-USA Solidarity Fellowship 2026: A Unique Human Rights Fellowship for Emerging Leaders Committed to Global Justice appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Are you passionate about human rights, social justice, advocacy, grassroots activism, and international solidarity? The PBI-USA Solidarity Fellowship offers an exceptional opportunity for emerging leaders to deepen their understanding of human rights protection while supporting the work of frontline human rights defenders around the world.

The Solidarity Fellowship is a leadership and advocacy initiative organized by Peace Brigades International-USA (PBI-USA), an organization dedicated to protecting human rights defenders and promoting nonviolent approaches to conflict transformation. Through this fellowship, participants gain hands-on experience in advocacy, communications, movement-building, and international solidarity while contributing to the protection of activists facing threats and violence in some of the world’s most challenging environments.

The fellowship is designed for individuals who are committed to advancing justice, amplifying marginalized voices, and supporting communities working to defend human rights, environmental justice, land rights, indigenous rights, and democratic freedoms.

About Peace Brigades International-USA (PBI-USA)

Peace Brigades International (PBI) is an internationally recognized non-governmental organization founded in 1981 with a mission to protect human rights and promote the nonviolent transformation of conflicts. For more than four decades, PBI has worked alongside threatened human rights defenders, providing protective accompaniment and international support that helps create safer environments for activists and civil society organizations.

PBI’s work spans multiple countries where human rights defenders face risks due to their efforts to promote justice, accountability, environmental protection, indigenous rights, gender equality, and peacebuilding. The organization’s presence and international networks help deter violence and create space for local activists to continue their important work.

The Solidarity Fellowship reflects PBI-USA’s commitment to building a new generation of human rights advocates who understand the importance of international solidarity and grassroots action.

What is the PBI-USA Solidarity Fellowship?

The Solidarity Fellowship is a professional development and leadership opportunity that allows fellows to engage directly with human rights advocacy efforts while learning about international human rights protection mechanisms.

The fellowship provides participants with practical experience and exposure to:

  • Human rights advocacy
  • International solidarity campaigns
  • Communications and storytelling
  • Public education and awareness initiatives
  • Community organizing
  • Policy engagement
  • Nonviolent conflict transformation
  • Movement-building strategies
  • Human rights accompaniment models

Fellows become part of a network of activists, advocates, and organizations dedicated to advancing human rights and supporting communities at risk.

Why the Fellowship Matters

Across the world, human rights defenders often face intimidation, criminalization, threats, surveillance, and violence because of their work.

Many defenders advocate for:

  • Indigenous rights
  • Women’s rights
  • Environmental protection
  • Land rights
  • Community development
  • Freedom of expression
  • Access to justice
  • Democratic participation

PBI’s work focuses on creating safer environments for these defenders by mobilizing international attention and solidarity. Through the fellowship, participants gain a deeper understanding of the challenges faced by activists globally and learn how international networks can contribute to their protection.

Fellowship Learning Areas

Participants in the Solidarity Fellowship can expect to strengthen their knowledge and skills in several key areas.

1. Human Rights Advocacy

Fellows learn how advocacy campaigns are developed and implemented to support threatened communities and human rights defenders.

2. Strategic Communications

The programme helps participants understand how storytelling, media engagement, and public education can amplify important human rights issues.

3. International Solidarity

Fellows gain insight into how global networks collaborate to support local movements and protect activists facing risks.

4. Nonviolent Peacebuilding

Participants explore approaches to conflict transformation that prioritize dialogue, protection, and community-led solutions.

5. Leadership Development

The fellowship helps emerging leaders strengthen their communication, collaboration, and organizational skills.

6. Global Human Rights Issues

Participants deepen their understanding of international human rights frameworks and contemporary human rights challenges affecting communities worldwide.

Benefits of the PBI-USA Solidarity Fellowship

The fellowship offers numerous professional and personal development benefits.

Professional Experience

Participants gain meaningful experience within an internationally respected human rights organization.

Mentorship and Networking

Fellows have opportunities to engage with experienced advocates, human rights defenders, and professionals working across the human rights sector.

Leadership Development

The programme helps participants build leadership skills that can support future careers in advocacy, nonprofit management, public policy, law, international development, and social justice.

Human Rights Knowledge

Participants develop a stronger understanding of international human rights systems and grassroots organizing strategies.

Career Advancement

The experience gained through the fellowship can strengthen future applications for graduate studies, advocacy roles, nonprofit positions, and international development opportunities.

Who Should Apply?

The fellowship is ideal for:

  • Emerging human rights advocates
  • Students and recent graduates
  • Social justice organizers
  • Community leaders
  • Aspiring nonprofit professionals
  • Individuals interested in international affairs
  • Peacebuilding practitioners
  • Human rights researchers
  • Advocacy and communications professionals

Applicants who are passionate about justice, human rights, and global solidarity are particularly encouraged to apply.

How to Apply

Interested applicants should visit the official PBI-USA Solidarity Fellowship webpage to review current opportunities, eligibility requirements, application instructions, and programme details.

Applicants are encouraged to prepare strong application materials that clearly demonstrate their commitment to human rights, advocacy, social justice, and community engagement.

Why You Should Consider This Fellowship

The PBI-USA Solidarity Fellowship is more than a professional opportunity. It is an opportunity to contribute to a global movement that protects human rights defenders and supports communities working for justice and peace.

For individuals seeking practical advocacy experience, leadership development, and a deeper understanding of international human rights work, the fellowship offers a powerful platform to learn, grow, and make a meaningful impact.

The post PBI-USA Solidarity Fellowship 2026: A Unique Human Rights Fellowship for Emerging Leaders Committed to Global Justice appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Mental Health America Youth Policy Accelerator 2026–2027: Earn a $3,000 Stipend While Shaping Youth Mental Health Policy in the United States
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
Applications Open for Mental Health America’s Prestigious Youth Policy Accelerator (YPA) 2026–2027 Young leaders passionate about mental health advocacy, youth empowerment, public policy, peer support, and systems change now have an exciting opportunity to influence mental health policy at the national level. Mental Health America (MHA) has officially opened applications for its 2026–2027 Youth Policy […]

The post Mental Health America Youth Policy Accelerator 2026–2027: Earn a $3,000 Stipend While Shaping Youth Mental Health Policy in the United States appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications Open for Mental Health America’s Prestigious Youth Policy Accelerator (YPA) 2026–2027

Young leaders passionate about mental health advocacy, youth empowerment, public policy, peer support, and systems change now have an exciting opportunity to influence mental health policy at the national level.

Mental Health America (MHA) has officially opened applications for its 2026–2027 Youth Policy Accelerator (YPA), a highly competitive youth leadership program designed to place young people’s voices and priorities at the center of mental health policy discussions.

The program will select five exceptional young adults from across the United States and provide them with training, mentorship, policy development experience, networking opportunities, and a $3,000 stipend while working on advancing youth peer support policy.

Participants will gain hands-on experience collaborating with experts, advocates, policymakers, and youth leaders while helping shape the future of mental health services for young people nationwide.

What is the Mental Health America Youth Policy Accelerator?

The Youth Policy Accelerator is a national youth leadership initiative developed by Mental Health America to ensure that young people have meaningful opportunities to influence mental health policies that directly affect their lives and communities.

The 2026–2027 cohort will focus specifically on:

State Youth Peer Support Policy

Youth peer support is increasingly recognized as a powerful tool for improving mental health outcomes among young people. The YPA seeks to strengthen and expand access to peer support programs by equipping participants with the knowledge, skills, and relationships needed to advocate for effective policy change.

Participants will help develop youth-centered policy recommendations that can be used by advocates, organizations, and decision-makers across the United States.

Program Structure

The Youth Policy Accelerator consists of two major phases.

Part I: Policy Development and Research

July 2026 – September 2026

During the first phase, participants will work together to develop model state legislation and policy recommendations related to youth peer support.

Activities include:

  • Learning about youth peer support policy
  • Conducting policy research
  • Interviewing experts and stakeholders
  • Participating in leadership development activities
  • Co-authoring youth-driven policy recommendations
  • Building knowledge of legislative processes

Time commitment:

  • Approximately 2–4 hours per week

Part II: Advocacy and Policy Action

October 2026 – March 2027

The second phase focuses on turning ideas into action.

Participants will:

  • Publish policy recommendations
  • Collaborate with the National Youth Peer Support Coalition
  • Educate policymakers and advocates
  • Participate in national advocacy efforts
  • Support implementation strategies
  • Advance youth peer support policy nationwide

The phase includes:

  • Twice-monthly virtual check-ins
  • Ongoing mentorship
  • Policy engagement opportunities
  • Public speaking experiences

Attend and Speak at a National Conference

One of the most exciting components of the program is the opportunity to participate in Mental Health America’s major national events.

Participants will attend and speak at:

Mental Health America Policy Institute and Annual Conference

Location:
Washington, D.C.

Dates:
October 6–9, 2026

This event brings together:

  • Policymakers
  • Researchers
  • Mental health professionals
  • Advocates
  • Youth leaders
  • Community organizations

Participants will share their work, engage with national leaders, and contribute to important policy conversations.

Financial Support

$3,000 Stipend

Each selected Youth Policy Accelerator member receives:

  • A $3,000 participation stipend

This compensation recognizes the value of participants’ time, expertise, and contributions.

Conference Scholarship

Participants will also receive full support to attend the Washington, D.C. conference.

Covered expenses include:

  • Travel
  • Lodging
  • Conference participation

Why Apply?

The Youth Policy Accelerator offers far more than a leadership title.

Participants gain practical skills, professional experience, and national-level exposure.

1. Build Real Policy Experience

Participants learn:

  • Policy analysis
  • Legislative development
  • Policy advocacy
  • Research methods
  • Coalition building

2. Develop Leadership Skills

The program strengthens:

  • Public speaking
  • Strategic communication
  • Facilitation
  • Teamwork
  • Advocacy leadership

3. Influence Mental Health Policy

Participants directly contribute to policy recommendations and advocacy strategies that may influence state-level systems.

4. Expand Your Professional Network

Members connect with:

  • Mental health experts
  • Policymakers
  • Researchers
  • Advocates
  • National youth leaders

5. Join a Lifelong Community

Participants become part of Mental Health America’s youth leadership network, which includes more than 150 alumni.

This network provides:

  • Continued training
  • Leadership opportunities
  • Mentorship
  • Professional development

Who Should Apply?

Mental Health America encourages applications from young adults with diverse experiences and backgrounds.

The organization is particularly interested in applicants who are passionate about improving mental health systems and expanding access to youth peer support.

Previous participants have backgrounds in:

  • Research
  • Public policy
  • Mental health advocacy
  • College programs
  • Peer support services
  • Community organizing
  • Youth leadership initiatives

The program values lived experience and diverse perspectives.

Application Requirements

Applicants must address the following topics:

Why Youth Peer Support Matters

Explain your interest in peer support and why it is important.

Experience with Peer Support Programs

Examples include:

  • Peer support initiatives
  • Mental health education programs
  • Online support communities
  • Crisis text lines
  • Certified peer specialist roles

Experience in Mental Health Advocacy or Policy

Share examples of policy, advocacy, leadership, or community engagement work.

What You Hope to Gain

Describe how the program aligns with your goals.

How You Will Contribute

Explain how you will support and enrich the experience of fellow participants.

Submission Formats

Applicants may submit:

  • Essays
  • Video responses
  • Bulleted responses

Written submissions:

  • 250–500 words

Video submissions:

  • 3–5 minutes

Applicants may also provide:

  • Academic papers
  • Articles
  • Websites
  • Blogs
  • Social media projects
  • Advocacy portfolios
  • Research work

Important Dates

Application Deadline:
July 17, 2026

Selection Notifications:
July 23, 2026

Program Duration:
July 2026 – March 2027

Conference Dates:
October 6–9, 2026

Contact Information

For questions about the application process or assistance completing the application:

Jackie Menjivar
Manager of Peer and Youth Advocacy
Mental Health America

Email:
jmenjivar@mhanational.org

Click HERE to learn more and apply!

Conclusion

The Mental Health America Youth Policy Accelerator 2026–2027 offers an exceptional opportunity for young advocates to gain practical policy experience, develop leadership skills, influence mental health systems, and join a national movement advancing youth peer support.

With a $3,000 stipend, national conference participation, mentorship, and direct engagement with policymakers and experts, the program provides an ideal platform for emerging leaders who want to create meaningful change in youth mental health policy.

For young adults committed to mental health advocacy and systems transformation, this opportunity represents a powerful pathway to leadership, impact, and long-term professional growth.

Visit Opportunities for Youth (OFY) to discover more global Fellowship, Health, and Accelerator opportunities.

The post Mental Health America Youth Policy Accelerator 2026–2027: Earn a $3,000 Stipend While Shaping Youth Mental Health Policy in the United States appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Creatives4Peace Youth Online Innovation Challenge by United Nations Development Programme (UNDP): Empowering Young African Creatives to Drive Peace, Dialogue, and Social Cohesion Through Art
item.dc:creator: Dennis Mwangi
item.description:
The Creatives4Peace Youth Online Innovation Challenge, organized by the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP), is a groundbreaking initiative designed to empower young African creatives who are using art, culture, and digital expression as tools for peacebuilding, dialogue, and social cohesion. This opportunity recognizes that creativity is not just a form of expression, but also a […]

The post Creatives4Peace Youth Online Innovation Challenge by United Nations Development Programme (UNDP): Empowering Young African Creatives to Drive Peace, Dialogue, and Social Cohesion Through Art appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Creatives4Peace Youth Online Innovation Challenge, organized by the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP), is a groundbreaking initiative designed to empower young African creatives who are using art, culture, and digital expression as tools for peacebuilding, dialogue, and social cohesion. This opportunity recognizes that creativity is not just a form of expression, but also a powerful force for transforming societies, bridging divides, and fostering unity across communities.

This challenge invites young innovators from across Africa to submit their creative projects in digital form. The goal is to highlight how artistic expression can be used to reduce conflict, encourage tolerance, and promote inclusive development in societies that are often divided by social, political, or cultural differences.

Selected participants will gain international visibility, professional exposure, and the opportunity to present their work at the Culture and Creative Industries Forum in Cameroon, where they will engage with industry leaders, investors, and fellow creatives from across Africa and the diaspora.

Overview of the Creatives4Peace Innovation Challenge

The Creatives4Peace initiative focuses on harnessing the power of creative industries to build peaceful and inclusive societies. It encourages submissions that demonstrate how art and creativity can influence positive social change.

Applicants are encouraged to submit work that can be shared digitally and that reflects strong themes of peacebuilding, inclusion, tolerance, and cultural unity.

Eligible Creative Fields

Participants may submit projects in any of the following creative and cultural industries:

1. Performing Arts

Projects may include:

  • Music compositions and performances
  • Short films and documentaries
  • Dance productions
  • Theatre and stage performances
  • Spoken word poetry and storytelling
  • Performance-based artistic works

2. Gaming and Interactive Media

This includes:

  • Video games and mobile games
  • Board games and physical games
  • Educational simulations and puzzles
  • Gamified learning tools
  • Interactive storytelling platforms

3. Digital Art and Multimedia

Projects may include:

  • Graphic design and illustration
  • Animation and motion graphics
  • Photography and videography
  • Digital storytelling projects
  • Multimedia installations and interactive visuals

4. Fashion and Wearable Design

This category includes:

  • Clothing and textile design
  • Sustainable fashion and upcycling
  • Costume design
  • Accessories and wearable art
  • Fashion-based creative enterprises

5. Crafts and Artisan Work

Eligible works include:

  • Handmade crafts and artisan products
  • Woodwork and ceramics
  • Jewelry design and beadwork
  • Textile weaving and traditional crafts
  • Painting and visual craft production

Benefits for Selected Participants

Selected creatives will receive significant opportunities, including:

  • Showcase of their work at the Culture and Creative Industries Forum in Cameroon
  • Fully funded travel and daily subsistence allowance (DSA) covered by UNDP
  • Opportunity to pitch creative projects to private sector partners for investment or collaboration
  • International exposure and visibility across creative industry platforms
  • Networking opportunities with young creatives from Africa and the diaspora
  • Access to future support, partnerships, and professional growth opportunities

Additionally, three outstanding participants will be selected for special recognition and invited to present their work at the forum in Cameroon.

Application Process

Interested applicants must submit their creative work through the official application form provided by UNDP. Submissions must be digital and clearly demonstrate how the creative work contributes to peacebuilding, social cohesion, and dialogue.

Applicants are encouraged to apply early and ensure their submissions clearly reflect originality, creativity, and social impact.

APPLY HERE

Deadline

The application deadline is September (exact date not specified, likely aligned with the September 2026 Culture and Creative Industries Forum in Cameroon). Applicants are strongly encouraged to apply as early as possible due to competitive selection.

For more information about this opportunity, visit here.

Discover more Global Competition Opportunities on OFY here.

The post Creatives4Peace Youth Online Innovation Challenge by United Nations Development Programme (UNDP): Empowering Young African Creatives to Drive Peace, Dialogue, and Social Cohesion Through Art appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Mastercard Foundation Associate Program East Africa 2026: 3,550 Job Placement Opportunities for Young Professionals Across Kenya, Uganda, Rwanda, Tanzania, Ethiopia, South Sudan, and Burundi
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
The Mastercard Foundation Associate Program East Africa 2026 is a large-scale youth employment and talent placement initiative designed to address one of the most pressing challenges in the region: access to meaningful, structured, and dignified employment opportunities for young people. The program is implemented in partnership with the global philanthropic organization the Mastercard Foundation and […]

The post Mastercard Foundation Associate Program East Africa 2026: 3,550 Job Placement Opportunities for Young Professionals Across Kenya, Uganda, Rwanda, Tanzania, Ethiopia, South Sudan, and Burundi appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Mastercard Foundation Associate Program East Africa 2026 is a large-scale youth employment and talent placement initiative designed to address one of the most pressing challenges in the region: access to meaningful, structured, and dignified employment opportunities for young people.

The program is implemented in partnership with the global philanthropic organization the Mastercard Foundation and supported by placement and recruitment partners including Shortlist Futures. Together, these organizations aim to create a structured pathway that connects skilled and semi-skilled young Africans to real job opportunities across multiple industries.

This initiative is not just a recruitment exercise. It is a full employment pipeline that includes outreach, screening, skills training, employer matching, interviews, and final job contracting.

The program targets the placement of 3,550 young Africans into jobs across Kenya, Uganda, Rwanda, Tanzania, Ethiopia, South Sudan, and Burundi, making it one of the most significant regional youth employment programs currently underway.

Purpose and Long-Term Vision of the Program

The Mastercard Foundation Associate Program was developed with a long-term development objective: to reduce youth unemployment by building sustainable career pathways.

Its vision is anchored on four major goals:

1. Expanding Access to Employment

Many young people in East Africa struggle to secure formal employment due to lack of networks, experience, or access to opportunities. This program removes those barriers by directly connecting candidates to employers.

2. Strengthening Work Readiness

The program provides structured training to ensure candidates are not only employable but also workplace-ready.

3. Promoting Inclusive Employment

A strong focus is placed on:

  • Women
  • Persons with disabilities (PWDs)
  • Refugees
  • Youth from underserved communities

4. Bridging the Skills Gap

The program ensures that candidates are equipped with modern job-market skills, especially in areas such as:

  • Digital literacy
  • Artificial intelligence in job searching
  • Interview preparation
  • Career management

Countries Covered Under the Program

This initiative covers seven East African countries:

  • Kenya
  • Uganda
  • Rwanda
  • Tanzania
  • Ethiopia
  • South Sudan
  • Burundi

Candidates from these countries are eligible to apply, and roles may be located locally or internationally depending on employer requirements.

Types of Job Opportunities Available

The Associate Program does not limit applicants to a single industry. Instead, it connects candidates to a wide range of sectors depending on employer demand.

Common sectors include:

1. Business and Administration

  • Administrative assistants
  • Office coordinators
  • Operations associates
  • Customer service roles

2. Technology and Digital Roles

  • IT support
  • Data entry and analysis
  • Junior software-related roles
  • Digital operations support

3. Finance and Accounting

  • Accounting assistants
  • Finance trainees
  • Bookkeeping support roles

4. Customer Experience and Sales

  • Customer support representatives
  • Sales associates
  • Call center agents

5. Development and NGO Sector Roles

  • Program assistants
  • Field support officers
  • Community engagement roles

The flexibility of the program ensures that candidates from different academic and professional backgrounds can find suitable roles.

Candidate Journey: Step-by-Step Process Explained in Detail

The program follows a structured and transparent five-stage recruitment pipeline designed to ensure fairness and efficiency.

Stage 1: Outreach and Application Submission

Candidates begin by filling out the Call for Application (CFA) form through the official recruitment system. This stage collects:

  • Personal information
  • Educational background
  • Skills and competencies
  • Career interests
  • Availability and work preferences

At this stage, accuracy and completeness are critical because the data is used for future matching.

Stage 2: Screening and Talent Pool Creation

Once applications are submitted, they enter a centralized talent pool managed by the recruitment team.

During screening:

  • Applications are reviewed for completeness
  • Candidate profiles are evaluated for alignment
  • Basic eligibility checks are conducted
  • Profiles are categorized based on skills and interests

Only candidates who meet the baseline criteria proceed to the next stage.

Stage 3: Training and Career Preparation (Prep2Soar Program)

Selected candidates are invited into the Prep2Soar training program, which is one of the most important stages of the entire process.

This training includes:

Career Self-Management

  • Understanding career paths
  • Setting professional goals
  • Building personal development plans

Interview Readiness

  • How to answer common interview questions
  • Professional communication skills
  • Confidence building techniques

Job Search in the Age of Artificial Intelligence

  • Using digital tools for job applications
  • Understanding AI-driven recruitment systems
  • Optimizing CVs for digital screening systems

Onboarding Preparation

  • Workplace expectations
  • Professional behavior and ethics
  • Understanding employer standards

This stage ensures that candidates are not just selected, but fully prepared for employment.

Stage 4: Shortlisting and Employer Matching

After training, candidates enter the shortlisting phase where they are matched to real job opportunities.

This includes:

  • Reviewing candidate profiles against employer needs
  • Inviting candidates to express interest in specific roles
  • Scheduling interviews with employers
  • Conducting assessments where required

This stage is highly competitive, as employers select candidates based on both skill and readiness.

Stage 5: Contracting and Employment Placement

Successful candidates receive job offers directly from employers.

At this stage:

  • Employment contracts are issued
  • Terms of employment are agreed upon
  • Candidates officially become associates under the program
  • Onboarding into the workplace begins

This marks the completion of the program’s pipeline and the start of formal employment.

Key Benefits of the Program

Participants in the Mastercard Foundation Associate Program gain multiple advantages beyond employment.

Employment Access

Direct connection to verified employers across East Africa.

Professional Training

Access to structured career readiness modules.

Real Work Experience

Opportunities to build practical, job-relevant experience.

Career Development

Exposure to multiple industries and long-term career pathways.

Inclusive Opportunity

Equal access for youth regardless of background, with special focus on marginalized groups.

Flexible Job Types

Remote, hybrid, and onsite opportunities depending on employer requirements.

Important Application Guidance

Applicants should take note of the following:

  • All assessment questions must be completed fully
  • Incomplete applications may not be considered
  • Accuracy in responses increases chances of matching
  • Candidates already applied between March and May 2026 should not reapply
  • The system uses responses for both screening and employer matching

APPLY HERE

Deadline Information

There is no fixed application deadline. The program operates on a rolling basis, meaning applications are reviewed continuously. However, early application is strongly recommended because opportunities are filled as suitable candidates are identified.

For more information about this opportunity, visit here.

Discover more Global Internship Opportunities on OFY here.

The post Mastercard Foundation Associate Program East Africa 2026: 3,550 Job Placement Opportunities for Young Professionals Across Kenya, Uganda, Rwanda, Tanzania, Ethiopia, South Sudan, and Burundi appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations Communication Officer (Science and Agrifood Innovation) Job Opportunity in Rome, Italy
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO) is inviting applications for the position of Communication Officer (Science and Agrifood Innovation) based at its headquarters in Rome, Italy. This exciting international career opportunity offers professionals with expertise in strategic communications, science communication, agriculture, food security, and innovation the chance to contribute to global […]

The post Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations Communication Officer (Science and Agrifood Innovation) Job Opportunity in Rome, Italy appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO) is inviting applications for the position of Communication Officer (Science and Agrifood Innovation) based at its headquarters in Rome, Italy. This exciting international career opportunity offers professionals with expertise in strategic communications, science communication, agriculture, food security, and innovation the chance to contribute to global efforts aimed at transforming agrifood systems and advancing sustainable development.

The successful candidate will join the Office of the Chief Scientist and play a critical role in enhancing the visibility and impact of FAO’s scientific work, ensuring that evidence-based knowledge and innovation reach policymakers, stakeholders, scientific communities, and the public.

Application Deadline

Deadline: 26 June 2026

Applicants are strongly encouraged to submit their applications well before the closing date.

About the Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO)

The Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations is a specialized agency of the United Nations dedicated to achieving food security, improving nutrition, promoting sustainable agriculture, and supporting rural development worldwide.

Through its Strategic Framework 2022–2031, FAO works to create:

  • More efficient agrifood systems
  • More inclusive food systems
  • More resilient agricultural systems
  • More sustainable food production practices

The organization seeks to support better production, better nutrition, a better environment, and a better life for all while ensuring that no one is left behind.

Position Overview

Job Title

Communication Officer (Science and Agrifood Innovation)

Location

Rome, Italy

Organizational Unit

Office of the Chief Scientist (DDCC)

Grade Level

P-3

Job Type

Professional Staff Position

Contract Duration

Fixed-term appointment for two years, with the possibility of extension

Reporting Line

The Communication Officer reports directly to the Chief Scientist and collaborates closely with FAO’s Office of Communications.

The Role and Its Importance

Science, technology, and innovation are at the heart of modern solutions for global food security, climate resilience, sustainable agriculture, and nutrition challenges.

The Communication Officer will help bridge the gap between scientific knowledge and public understanding by translating complex scientific information into accessible, engaging, and impactful communication products. The role supports FAO’s mission to strengthen its position as a trusted source of scientific information and a neutral platform for global discussions on agrifood systems.

Key Responsibilities

The selected candidate will undertake a broad range of communication and outreach activities, including:

Strategic Communication Planning

  • Support communication and advocacy strategies for science, technology, and innovation initiatives.
  • Develop audience-focused communication approaches.
  • Increase awareness of FAO’s scientific contributions to food security and sustainable development.
  • Ensure communication activities align with FAO’s strategic priorities.

Content Development

The officer will create and edit a variety of high-quality communication materials, including:

  • Articles
  • Briefing notes
  • Reports
  • Statements
  • Speeches
  • Presentations
  • Talking points
  • Opinion pieces
  • Web content
  • Communication campaigns

All content must be tailored to specific audiences and supported by evidence-based messaging.

Science Communication and Storytelling

The role requires transforming technical and scientific information into engaging stories that resonate with diverse audiences.

Responsibilities include:

  • Promoting science communication best practices.
  • Supporting multilingual communication efforts.
  • Ensuring communications are culturally sensitive and inclusive.
  • Developing content that can reach audiences with limited digital access.

Event Management and Stakeholder Engagement

The Communication Officer will:

  • Support conferences, workshops, webinars, and international events.
  • Collaborate with scientists, researchers, policymakers, and development partners.
  • Facilitate communication activities during scientific meetings.
  • Strengthen outreach within scientific communities.

Digital Communication and Innovation

The successful candidate will contribute to modern communication approaches by:

  • Utilizing data-driven communication strategies.
  • Supporting responsible use of artificial intelligence in communication.
  • Producing mobile-first communication products.
  • Creating multimedia content such as:
    • Videos
    • Podcasts
    • Audio materials
    • Visual content
    • Radio programming

Media Relations and Risk Management

Responsibilities also include:

  • Monitoring media and communication trends.
  • Addressing misinformation risks.
  • Supporting media engagement efforts.
  • Managing reputational communication challenges.
  • Strengthening trusted communication channels.

Required Qualifications

To be eligible for this position, applicants must possess:

Educational Requirements

An advanced university degree (Master’s degree or equivalent) in one of the following areas:

  • Communication
  • Sustainable Agriculture
  • Environmental Science
  • Food Security and Nutrition
  • Climate Change
  • Related disciplines

Professional Experience

Applicants must have:

  • At least five years of relevant professional experience.
  • Experience in strategic communications.
  • Experience within agriculture, food security, nutrition, science, technology, innovation, or international development sectors.

Language Requirements

Candidates must demonstrate:

  • Working knowledge of English.
  • Intermediate knowledge of another FAO official language:
    • Arabic
    • Chinese
    • French
    • Russian
    • Spanish

Desired Skills and Competencies

FAO is seeking candidates who can demonstrate:

  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
  • Strong analytical and research abilities.
  • Experience handling technical reports and data.
  • Ability to communicate scientific information accurately.
  • Experience working in multicultural environments.
  • Stakeholder engagement skills.
  • Team collaboration capabilities.
  • Knowledge-sharing and continuous improvement mindset.

Additional advantages include:

  • Experience working internationally.
  • Familiarity with peer-review processes.
  • Understanding of FAO policies and programmes.
  • Experience covering international and multilateral affairs.
  • Knowledge of additional FAO languages.

Benefits and Compensation

FAO offers a highly competitive international employment package that may include:

  • Competitive United Nations salary package
  • Dependency allowances
  • Rental subsidy
  • Education grants for children
  • Home leave travel benefits
  • Thirty working days of annual leave per year
  • Pension benefits under the United Nations Joint Staff Pension Fund
  • International health insurance
  • Optional life insurance
  • Disability protection coverage

The organization also promotes:

  • Flexible working arrangements
  • Family-friendly workplace policies
  • Inclusive and diverse work environments
  • Professional development opportunities

FAO’s Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion

FAO values diversity and actively encourages applications from:

  • Women
  • Nationals of underrepresented member countries
  • Persons with disabilities
  • Professionals from diverse cultural and professional backgrounds

The organization maintains a zero-tolerance policy toward discrimination, harassment, abuse of authority, and misconduct.

Why This Opportunity Stands Out

This position offers a unique opportunity to work at the intersection of science, innovation, agriculture, communication, and international development.

Successful candidates will contribute directly to global efforts focused on:

  • Sustainable food systems
  • Climate resilience
  • Scientific innovation
  • Agricultural transformation
  • Global food security

Working at FAO headquarters in Rome provides exposure to high-level international policy discussions, global scientific networks, and opportunities to shape communication strategies that influence development outcomes worldwide.

How to Apply

Interested applicants should:

  1. Create or update their online profile on the FAO recruitment portal.
  2. Ensure all employment records, educational qualifications, and language skills are accurately listed.
  3. Prepare and upload a motivation letter.
  4. Complete and submit the online application before the deadline.

Applicants should ensure that all information provided is complete and accurate, as incomplete applications will not be considered.

APPLY HERE

Final Reminder

The application deadline for the Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations Communication Officer (Science and Agrifood Innovation) position is 26 June 2026. Interested professionals with expertise in communications, science, agriculture, and international development are encouraged to submit their applications early and take advantage of this prestigious opportunity to contribute to global agrifood transformation and sustainable development.

For more information about this opportunity, visit here.

Discover more Global Job Opportunities on OFY here.

The post Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations Communication Officer (Science and Agrifood Innovation) Job Opportunity in Rome, Italy appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Riley’s Way Foundation Junior Board 2026: Leadership Opportunity for Young Professionals Passionate About Kindness, Empathy, and Youth Impact
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
Young professionals who are passionate about creating meaningful change now have an exciting opportunity to contribute to a mission-driven organization dedicated to kindness, empathy, and youth leadership. The Riley’s Way Foundation Junior Board 2026 is seeking dynamic and committed young leaders from the tristate area who are eager to support youth-led social impact initiatives while […]

The post Riley’s Way Foundation Junior Board 2026: Leadership Opportunity for Young Professionals Passionate About Kindness, Empathy, and Youth Impact appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Young professionals who are passionate about creating meaningful change now have an exciting opportunity to contribute to a mission-driven organization dedicated to kindness, empathy, and youth leadership. The Riley’s Way Foundation Junior Board 2026 is seeking dynamic and committed young leaders from the tristate area who are eager to support youth-led social impact initiatives while developing their own leadership, fundraising, and community engagement skills.

This inaugural Junior Board represents a unique chance for emerging professionals to become ambassadors of positive change, helping Riley’s Way Foundation expand its impact and empower young changemakers across local, national, and global communities.

About Riley’s Way Foundation

Riley’s Way Foundation is a nonprofit organization that brings together passionate young changemakers between the ages of 13 and 23 who are committed to making their communities better and kinder places.

Through its leadership development programs, Riley’s Way helps young people transform ideas into meaningful action. The organization supports youth leaders through initiatives such as:

  • The Call For Kindness Fellowship
  • School-based Clubs
  • Youth Leadership Retreats

At the heart of Riley’s Way is the belief that empathy and kindness are essential leadership qualities. While traditional leadership programs often focus on technical skills and achievement, Riley’s Way emphasizes compassion, community engagement, and social impact.

Over the years, the organization has reached thousands of people through youth-led initiatives, demonstrating that kindness can be a powerful force for change.

What Is the Riley’s Way Junior Board?

The Riley’s Way Junior Board is a newly established leadership and fundraising body designed for young professionals aged 21 and above who want to support the organization’s mission.

Board members will play an active role in:

  • Advancing Riley’s Way’s mission
  • Supporting youth leadership programs
  • Strengthening fundraising efforts
  • Increasing community engagement
  • Serving as ambassadors for the organization

The Junior Board offers participants the opportunity to work alongside other passionate professionals while contributing to initiatives that empower young leaders to create positive social change.

Benefits of Joining the Riley’s Way Junior Board

Serving on the Junior Board provides numerous personal and professional benefits.

1. Leadership Development

Members gain practical leadership experience through:

  • Strategic engagement
  • Event planning
  • Community outreach
  • Nonprofit advocacy
  • Team collaboration

2. Professional Networking

Participants will connect with:

  • Fellow young professionals
  • Nonprofit leaders
  • Community advocates
  • Philanthropic partners
  • Youth development experts

3. Fundraising Experience

Board members will strengthen valuable fundraising skills through:

  • Event organization
  • Donor engagement
  • Campaign promotion
  • Community mobilization
  • Relationship building

4. Meaningful Community Impact

Members directly contribute to programs that empower young people to become leaders and changemakers in their communities.

5. Ambassador Opportunities

Junior Board members become representatives of Riley’s Way, helping spread awareness about the importance of kindness, empathy, and youth leadership.

6. Volunteer Engagement

Participants will have opportunities to volunteer and actively support Riley’s Way programs and events throughout their term.

Junior Board Responsibilities

Selected members will be expected to fulfill several commitments during their service.

Term Length

  • Serve a two-year term
  • Possibility of extension beyond the initial term

Monthly Commitment

  • Approximately two hours per month

Meetings

Members are expected to:

  • Participate in orientation sessions
  • Attend quarterly in-person meetings
  • Travel to New York City for meetings and events

Fundraising Requirements

Each member must:

  • Fulfill a minimum annual Give/Get contribution of $1,000
  • Host one fundraising event annually
  • Support additional Riley’s Way fundraising activities and events

Community Representation

Members will:

  • Act as Riley’s Way ambassadors
  • Promote organizational initiatives
  • Engage with community stakeholders
  • Advocate for youth leadership and kindness-centered change

Volunteer Participation

Board members are encouraged to actively volunteer and support organizational programming.

Application Requirements

Applicants will be asked to provide personal and professional information, including:

  • Contact information
  • Age and birthday
  • State and city of residence
  • Resume or curriculum vitae (CV)
  • LinkedIn profile

Applicants will also complete several short-answer questions designed to assess their leadership experience, commitment to kindness, fundraising potential, and interest in supporting Riley’s Way’s mission.

Key topics covered in the application include:

  • Motivation for joining the Junior Board
  • Understanding of kind leadership
  • Leadership experiences
  • Fundraising and community mobilization experience
  • Ability to attend in-person meetings in New York City
  • Previous participation in Riley’s Way programs (if applicable)

Applicants who have not previously participated in Riley’s Way programs are still encouraged to apply.

What Makes a Strong Applicant?

Competitive candidates will likely demonstrate:

  • Commitment to community service
  • Passion for youth empowerment
  • Strong communication skills
  • Leadership experience
  • Interest in nonprofit work
  • Fundraising or community engagement experience
  • Ability to collaborate effectively with diverse teams
  • Dedication to advancing kindness and empathy in leadership

Why This Opportunity Matters

Many nonprofit boards require years of experience before individuals can contribute at a leadership level. The Riley’s Way Junior Board creates a valuable pathway for emerging professionals to gain firsthand experience in nonprofit leadership while supporting programs that inspire the next generation of changemakers.

By joining the Junior Board, participants become part of a movement that recognizes kindness and empathy not as soft skills, but as essential qualities for effective leadership and sustainable social impact.

For young professionals seeking to grow their leadership abilities while making a meaningful difference, this opportunity offers a powerful platform to contribute, learn, and lead.

Application Information

Interested applicants should complete the Riley’s Way Junior Board 2026 application and prepare all required materials, including a resume/CV and LinkedIn profile.

For questions or additional information, applicants may contact:

Kate Sinclair
Email: ksinclair@rileysandler.org

APPLY HERE

Deadline

No application deadline has been specified. Interested candidates are encouraged to apply as early as possible.

For more information about this opportunity, visit here.

Discover more Global Fellowship Opportunities on OFY here.

The post Riley’s Way Foundation Junior Board 2026: Leadership Opportunity for Young Professionals Passionate About Kindness, Empathy, and Youth Impact appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Elevate Government Affairs Internship 2026: Paid Full-Time Government Affairs Internship in Federal Policy, Congress, Transportation, Technology, and Energy Policy
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
Are you passionate about federal policy, government relations, legislative affairs, transportation policy, technology regulation, telecommunications, or energy policy? Elevate Government Affairs is offering an exciting paid internship opportunity for aspiring government affairs professionals seeking hands-on experience in one of Washington’s most dynamic policy environments. This full-time, paid, and in-person internship provides participants with the opportunity […]

The post Elevate Government Affairs Internship 2026: Paid Full-Time Government Affairs Internship in Federal Policy, Congress, Transportation, Technology, and Energy Policy appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Are you passionate about federal policy, government relations, legislative affairs, transportation policy, technology regulation, telecommunications, or energy policy? Elevate Government Affairs is offering an exciting paid internship opportunity for aspiring government affairs professionals seeking hands-on experience in one of Washington’s most dynamic policy environments.

This full-time, paid, and in-person internship provides participants with the opportunity to work directly alongside experienced bipartisan government affairs professionals while gaining valuable exposure to congressional processes, policy analysis, client engagement, legislative tracking, and strategic advocacy.

The internship is ideal for recent graduates, bachelor’s degree holders, and emerging professionals who want to build a career in government affairs, public policy, lobbying, legislative affairs, political consulting, or public administration.

Successful candidates must be available to begin work on August 31, 2026, and remain in the role through December 18, 2026.

About Elevate Government Affairs

Elevate Government Affairs was established by three highly experienced government relations professionals with a vision of helping organizations successfully navigate an increasingly complex government affairs landscape.

The firm was created to address a growing challenge faced by organizations across sectors: ensuring their voices are heard in a policy environment that is becoming more competitive and complex.

Elevate’s mission centers on:

  • Delivering measurable policy outcomes for clients
  • Building customized government affairs strategies
  • Providing personalized client engagement
  • Creating innovative advocacy solutions
  • Supporting clients at every level of government
  • Leveraging bipartisan expertise to achieve policy goals

What distinguishes Elevate Government Affairs is the depth of experience within its leadership and professional team. Collectively, the firm’s professionals possess more than 125 years of combined experience working with:

  • Members of the U.S. House of Representatives
  • Members of the U.S. Senate
  • Congressional Committees
  • Executive Branch agencies
  • Public policy organizations
  • Government relations initiatives

The organization emphasizes bipartisan collaboration and strategic innovation, enabling clients to effectively engage policymakers and decision-makers.

Internship Overview

This internship offers a unique opportunity to gain practical experience in federal policy and government affairs while working directly with professionals who shape public policy strategies.

Interns will become integral members of a collaborative team and contribute to projects involving:

  • Transportation policy
  • Infrastructure policy
  • Technology policy
  • Telecommunications policy
  • Energy policy
  • Congressional affairs
  • Legislative analysis
  • Client engagement
